CFA Institute It also fixes the reporting currency, valuation time, inception date, benchmark, and whether results are gross or net of fees and pre-tax or after-tax. Investor.gov Those choices should be documented before viewing the result so the investor cannot improve the story by changing the measurement rule afterward. CFA Institute

Start with total return and clean cash flows

The monthly ledger begins by reconciling beginning and ending market values with purchases, sales, dividends, interest, fees, taxes, foreign exchange, transfers, contributions, and withdrawals. Investor.gov Trades occurring inside the measured portfolio are not external cash flows, while money entering or leaving the defined portfolio is external. GIPS Calculated ending wealth must reconcile to the broker or custodian statement before ratios built on the ledger can be trusted. Investor.gov

Price return for one period is (P1 − P0) / P0, while total return adds cash distributions to the numerator. S&P Dow Jones Indices An equity total-return index incorporates both price movement and reinvested dividend income, so comparing a dividend-reinvesting portfolio with a price-only index is structurally inconsistent. S&P Dow Jones Indices A performance presentation must explain how dividends and taxes are treated rather than leave the reader to infer them. Investor.gov

Multiple-period returns compound geometrically as the product of (1 + periodic return) minus one. GIPS Annualized geometric return or CAGR converts that compounded wealth change into an equivalent yearly rate over the stated number of years. GIPS An arithmetic average can misstate the investor’s wealth path because a gain and an equal-percentage loss do not cancel after compounding. GIPS

Fees and expenses reduce realized returns and leave less capital in the portfolio to compound. Investor.gov A meaningful report should therefore show net-of-fee results and state whether taxes, withholding, transaction costs, and inflation are included or excluded. Investor.gov

Time-weighted return: evaluate the process

Time-weighted return is designed to remove the effect of external cash flows when evaluating an investment process. GIPS A true TWR splits the record at each external flow, calculates the investment return for each intervening subperiod, and geometrically links those subperiod returns. GIPS Deposits and withdrawals therefore do not mechanically improve or damage the reported strategy return. GIPS

TWR is the appropriate headline when comparing a manager, fund, asset-allocation rule, or active sleeve across investors whose external cash flows occur at different times. GIPS The return periods and valuation points around material flows still need accurate data, because a cash-flow-neutral formula cannot correct a wrong portfolio value. GIPS

Money-weighted return: measure the investor’s experience

Money-weighted return incorporates both the size and timing of external cash flows. GIPS It is the internal rate of return that sets the present value of dated contributions, withdrawals, and terminal value to zero, with contributions treated as investor outflows and withdrawals as inflows. GIPS A spreadsheet XIRR function can solve the irregular-date version when the signs and dates are recorded consistently. GIPS

MWR rises above TWR when relatively more capital was invested before strong subperiods and falls below TWR when more capital preceded weak subperiods. GIPS It is therefore the economically relevant measure for personal goal progress and for strategies in which the decision maker controls capital calls or withdrawals. GIPS A recurring-contribution investor should report both TWR and MWR because the first evaluates the portfolio process and the second captures the timing of the investor’s actual money. GIPS

Comparing a personal MWR directly with a public index’s TWR confounds cash-flow timing with investment performance. GIPS The two numbers can be shown together only when their different questions are clearly labeled. GIPS

Choose the benchmark before seeing the score

An appropriate benchmark should be selected before evaluation and should be investable, accessible, independent, and relevant to the mandate. CFA Institute A benchmark from a different market segment or investment type is not a fair apples-to-apples comparison. Investor.gov The comparison should match asset mix, geography, capitalization and style, factor exposures, currency and hedge policy, dividend convention, feasible risk, and the same start and end dates. CFA Institute

A balanced stock, bond, and cash portfolio needs a blended policy benchmark rather than the S&P 500 or another single-country equity index alone. Investor.gov A concentrated active sleeve should be evaluated against its own opportunity set instead of borrowing the diversified household portfolio’s easier benchmark. CFA Institute

Index labels require variant control because providers publish price, total-return, currency, hedged, and other derived versions of what appears to be the same market benchmark. S&P Dow Jones Indices Portfolio and benchmark should use compatible gross or net dividend conventions, currency treatment, and fee or tax bases. S&P Dow Jones Indices Benchmark changes should apply prospectively and be documented rather than chosen after the period to make a result appear better. CFA Institute

U.S. fund shareholder reports illustrate a minimum retail comparison standard by presenting average annual total returns for one-, five-, and ten-year periods alongside an appropriate broad-based market index. Investor.gov A personal dashboard can extend that discipline with inception results, rolling 12-, 36-, and 60-month windows, and down-market or regime panels. Investor.gov

Add risk to the return story

Volatility measures the dispersion of periodic returns, while downside deviation measures outcomes below a specified minimum acceptable return. Sharpe CFA Institute Risk and portfolio returns must use identical dates, frequencies, and conventions before ratios are compared. Sharpe

The historical Sharpe ratio is average differential return per unit of the differential return’s variability, using the mean and standard deviation of the same differential-return series. Sharpe Its differential is commonly the portfolio return minus a risk-free or other relevant reference return, and annualization must be internally consistent. Sharpe Sharpe alone does not incorporate the strategy’s correlation with the investor’s other holdings, so a higher standalone ratio does not automatically imply a better whole portfolio. Sharpe

The Sortino ratio replaces total standard deviation and the risk-free reference with downside deviation and a chosen minimum acceptable return. CFA Institute It avoids penalizing upside variation in the same way as downside variation, but comparisons are fair only when they use the same minimum acceptable return and compatible denominator methods. CFA Institute CFA guidance treats Sortino as useful but incomplete because target choice, limited downside observations, and other risks still affect interpretation. CFA Institute

Maximum drawdown is the largest observed cumulative loss from a prior peak to a subsequent trough. Goldberg and Mahmoud A practical report should record the peak date, trough date, recovery date, and time under water in addition to the percentage depth. Goldberg and Mahmoud Drawdown makes the path and recovery burden visible but remains backward-looking and sensitive to the chosen sample window. Goldberg and Mahmoud

Active return is the portfolio return minus the benchmark return for a period, while tracking error is the standard deviation of active returns. CFA Institute The information ratio relates average active return to that tracking error and should be read alongside drawdown, factor exposures, fees, and rolling behavior rather than as a stand-alone verdict. CFA Institute

Explain active return with attribution

A complete workflow includes benchmark selection, excess return, performance attribution, and risk analysis. CFA Institute Brinson-Fachler equity attribution decomposes active return into allocation, security selection, and interaction effects. CFA Institute Allocation measures the effect of overweighting or underweighting benchmark segments, selection measures the segment-level return difference, and interaction captures the cross-product of weight and return differences. CFA Institute

One-period effects should reconcile to active return, while arithmetic effects cannot simply be summed across time because compounded-return differences do not equal the sum of periodic differences. CFA Institute Multiperiod reporting therefore needs a documented linking convention and consistent treatment of interaction, currency, fees, and residuals. CFA Institute Transactions-based attribution can reconcile to portfolio return but requires complete and accurate holdings and transaction data. CFA Institute

Attribution explains the accounting sources of realized active return rather than proving repeatable skill. CFA Institute The 1991 Brinson update examined 82 large pension plans and found that active sponsor and manager decisions added little on average in its ten-year sample. Brinson, Singer, and Beebower That sample does not imply that active decisions never matter, because attribution depends on the benchmark, classification, weights, period, factor exposure, costs, and data quality. CFA Institute

The final review should separate strategic allocation, active tilts, security choice, investor-flow timing, implementation costs, factor and currency exposure, and luck. CFA Institute A favorable short outcome is not proof of skill, and a sound process should not be discarded solely because one short period was weak. CFA Institute

Korea benchmark implementation

KRX offers both total-return and net-total-return U.S.-dollar variants of KOSPI 200, demonstrating that benchmark variant selection matters for Korean investors. KRX A Korean performance record should preserve the exact index code and identify whether the comparator is price return, gross total return, net total return, KRW, USD, or currency-hedged. KRX The portfolio’s dividend withholding, taxes, fees, and foreign-exchange treatment must then be reconciled to that index convention before active return is interpreted. S&P Dow Jones Indices

A repeatable monthly dashboard

  1. Fix the measured portfolio, reporting currency, valuation time, inception date, fee and tax basis, and benchmark. Investor.gov
  2. Reconcile beginning value, ending value, every external flow, distributions, fees, taxes, FX, and internal trades to the statement. GIPS
  3. Calculate total return, compound periodic returns geometrically, and avoid mixing price-only and total-return series. S&P Dow Jones Indices
  4. Report TWR for the investment process and MWR for the investor experience with clearly labeled cash-flow conventions. GIPS
  5. Compare portfolio and benchmark over identical dates using the same currency, dividend, fee, and tax basis. Investor.gov
  6. Add volatility, Sharpe, Sortino, maximum drawdown and recovery, active return, tracking error, and rolling-period panels. Sharpe CFA Institute
  7. Attribute active return to allocation, selection, interaction, factors, currency, fees, and implementation only where data and a credible benchmark support the decomposition. CFA Institute
  8. Review decisions and methodology changes prospectively while retaining prior benchmark definitions and results. CFA Institute

Tensions:

  • TWR versus MWR as the headline. TWR removes external cash-flow effects and is fairer for evaluating the manager, strategy, or allocation process, while MWR incorporates the timing and size of the investor’s actual deposits and withdrawals. GIPS They answer different questions, so a recurring-contribution investor should report both and avoid comparing personal MWR directly with a TWR index. GIPS
  • Sharpe versus Sortino. Sharpe summarizes differential return per unit of total differential-return variability, while Sortino measures return above a chosen minimum acceptable return per unit of downside deviation. Sharpe CFA Institute Sortino avoids penalizing upside deviation but is sensitive to the target and denominator method, while Sharpe misses correlation with the rest of the investor’s holdings. CFA Institute Sharpe
  • Attribution versus proof of skill. Brinson attribution reconciles active return to allocation, selection, and interaction, but the result depends on benchmark, classification, weights, data, linking, and interaction conventions. CFA Institute It is decision accounting for realized return rather than a statistical demonstration of persistence after risk, costs, and factor controls. CFA Institute
  • Headline index versus policy benchmark. A broad index is transparent and useful as a secondary market reference, but a single-country price index mismatches a diversified portfolio containing bonds, global equity, cash, currency policy, and active sleeves. Investor.gov The primary comparator should be a predeclared blended total-return benchmark whose exposures and reporting convention match the investable mandate. CFA Institute

Unresolved questions:

  • What Sharpe or Sortino comparison set is appropriate for the portfolio’s asset class, benchmark, horizon, sample size, leverage, return smoothing, and distribution? ⚠️
  • How should after-tax household return be standardized across the investor’s Korean account types, foreign withholding regimes, and changing tax rules? ⚠️
  • Which professional conventions should govern derivatives collateral, options, private valuations, and multicurrency attribution for this specific mandate? ⚠️

9. Investor psychology & behavior

The behavioral layer — individual biases, process discipline, and collective/social dynamics — that determines whether any method is executed well.

9.1 Behavioral biases

Behavioral biases are recurring errors in judgment that can cause investors to act against their own financial interests. Source They are not confined to inexperienced investors, because the underlying problems involve ordinary reasoning shortcuts and emotional responses rather than a simple lack of intelligence. Source

The CFA Institute separates biases into cognitive errors, which arise from faulty reasoning or information processing, and emotional biases, which are driven by feelings. Source Cognitive errors are generally easier to correct, while emotional biases may require adapting the investment process around the response rather than expecting it to disappear. Source

The real-world cost appears in the behavior gap between the return of an investment and the return actually captured by investor cash flows. Source Over the decade ended December 2024, the average dollar invested in funds and ETFs earned about 7.0% annually while the funds themselves returned 8.2%, a gap of roughly 1.2 percentage points per year and about 15% of aggregate returns. Source

Morningstar found that volatility damaged investor outcomes more than fees because volatile funds provoked more erratic buying and selling. Source The result turns behavioral discipline from a soft personality topic into a measurable component of realized return. Source

Loss aversion and the disposition effect

Prospect-theory research describes investors as evaluating gains and losses relative to a reference point rather than only final wealth. Source ⚠️ The familiar claim that losses hurt about 2.25 times as much as equivalent gains came from a sample of only 25 highly educated graduate students, so the precision and universality of that coefficient are contested. Source ⚠️

The broader framework has stronger cross-cultural support than the exact coefficient. Source ⚠️ A multinational replication involving 4,098 participants across 19 countries and 13 languages replicated 94% of the tested prospect-theory items, although some effects were attenuated. Source ⚠️

In investing, loss aversion contributes to the disposition effect: selling winners too early while holding losing positions too long. Source Shefrin and Statman coined the term in 1985 and connected the behavior with loss aversion, mental accounting, regret avoidance, and self-control. Source

Odean’s brokerage data showed that a stock trading above its purchase price was almost 60% more likely to be sold than one trading below it. Source Outside December, gains were realized 14.8% of the time compared with 9.8% for losses, while the pattern reversed in December because of tax-loss harvesting. Source

The disposition effect can be measured by comparing the Proportion of Gains Realized with the Proportion of Losses Realized. Source A PGR-to-PLR ratio above 1.0 indicates that the investor realizes winning positions more readily than losing positions. Source

This diagnostic is more useful than asking whether the investor feels loss-averse. Source A trade log can reveal whether losing positions are repeatedly retained because the thesis remains sound or merely because realizing a loss would make the error emotionally final. Source Source

Overconfidence and excessive trading

Overconfidence can explain unusually high trading levels and the poor resulting net performance of individual investors. Source The behavioral mechanism is straightforward: an investor who overestimates the quality of personal information or forecasting skill finds too many reasons to trade. Source

In Barber and Odean’s brokerage data, the households that traded most earned 11.4% annually while the market returned 17.9%. Source The average household earned 16.4% while turning over about 75% of its portfolio each year. Source

Gender differences in the same research provide another overconfidence test. Source Men traded 45% more than women and earned risk-adjusted net returns about 1.4 percentage points lower per year; among single investors, men traded 67% more and earned about 2.3 points less. Source

Turnover is therefore a practical warning metric. Source Source A high rate does not prove that every trade was irrational, but it forces the investor to show that added decisions overcame transaction costs and the empirical link between excessive activity and lower net returns. Source Source

Overconfidence can also reduce diversification when the investor believes a small set of personal ideas is unusually reliable. Source The same process becomes self-reinforcing when greater study produces greater subjective certainty without a corresponding improvement in forecasting accuracy. Source

Anchoring, recency, and herding

Anchoring is the tendency to rely too heavily on an initial piece of information and adjust too little when new evidence arrives. Source ⚠️ In a portfolio, the anchor may be the purchase price, a prior high, or an analyst target, none of which determines the security’s current economic value. Source ⚠️

Anchoring interacts with the disposition effect when an investor refuses to sell until a losing stock returns to the entry price. Source Source ⚠️ The useful question is whether new evidence preserves the investment thesis, not whether the market price has returned to an emotionally important number. Source ⚠️

Recency bias is the tendency to give excessive weight to recent experience or the latest information when estimating future events. Source ⚠️ It can turn a recent rally into an expectation of continued gains or a recent drawdown into an assumption that losses will persist, encouraging performance chasing and panic. Source ⚠️

Herding occurs when investors follow the crowd rather than perform independent analysis, amplifying market swings and contributing to bubbles and crashes. Source ⚠️ Social proof and fear of missing out can reinforce recency because the most recent winners are also the investments receiving the most visible attention. Source Source ⚠️

One low-quality source cited in the node claimed that 75% of retail meme-stock investors lost money in a 2024 study and connected the 2025 resurgence with recency, herding, and FOMO. Source ⚠️ Because the source is graded unreliable with low confidence, the exact loss rate should be treated as anecdotal rather than established evidence. Source ⚠️

Korea-specific evidence

Research on Korean individual investors finds a clear disposition effect, with a higher probability of selling a stock that has risen since purchase than one that has fallen. Source ⚠️ The same evidence reports annual Korean individual-investor turnover above 1,600% after the 2020 COVID-era surge and performance below market returns. Source ⚠️

The Korea Capital Market Institute identifies four recurring retail patterns: overconfidence, treating stocks as jackpot opportunities, short-term collective trading, and the disposition effect. Source ⚠️ These categories connect the Korean evidence to the same mechanisms observed in the broader trading and disposition-effect literature. Source Source ⚠️

The Korean findings should be used as a diagnostic rather than as a stereotype about every retail investor. Source ⚠️ Their practical value is to make turnover, lottery-like concentration, crowd-following, and realized-gain-versus-loss patterns visible in the investor’s own records. Source ⚠️

Debiasing as process design

Recognizing and detecting a bias is the first step in reducing its effect on financial decisions. Source The CFA framework calls reducing or eliminating the bias within the individual “moderating,” while a process can instead adapt around an emotional bias that is unlikely to disappear. Source

A 2024 experiment found that informing investors about the disposition effect and its consequences significantly reduced the bias. Source The reduction came from a greater willingness to realize losing stocks, not from a change in the tendency to sell winners. Source

The improvement persisted at two weeks but had diminished to statistical non-significance by three months. Source Debiasing education therefore appears real but temporary, which implies that useful interventions need reinforcement rather than one-time exposure. Source

Structural guardrails reduce the number of decisions that must be made during stress. Source Source A written investment policy, predetermined buy and sell rules, a decision journal, checklists, pre-mortems, scheduled rebalancing, regular contributions, and automation can move choices from an emotional moment into a calmer design stage. Source Source

A decision journal should record the thesis, disconfirming evidence, expected time horizon, sell conditions, and emotional state at the time of the trade. Source This creates an auditable record that cannot be rewritten as easily by hindsight after the outcome is known. Source

Automation and rules-based investing can act as debiasing devices because they remove repeated opportunities for fear, excitement, and overconfidence to alter the plan. Source The behavior-gap evidence makes that reduction in discretion economically meaningful when volatile markets would otherwise provoke erratic cash flows. Source

Debiasing should not become a mechanical command to reverse every intuitive response. Source The same 2024 study found that, under mean-reverting market conditions, a higher disposition effect was associated with higher returns, so reducing the bias in the wrong context could hurt performance. Source

The goal is a repeatable decision process rather than behavioral purity. Source Bias labels should prompt evidence checks, position-size limits, and predetermined rules without pathologizing every profitable or unprofitable decision after the fact. Source Source

Tensions:

  • How robust is loss aversion? The famous 2.25 coefficient is fragile as a universal estimate because it came from only 25 graduate students making hypothetical choices. Source ⚠️ The broader prospect-theory pattern replicated across 4,098 participants in 19 countries, with 94% of items reproduced despite some attenuation. Source ⚠️ The evidence supports reference-dependent behavior more strongly than it supports one permanent numerical coefficient for every investor and context. Source Source ⚠️

  • Does debiasing work? Information about the disposition effect significantly reduced it by increasing the willingness to sell losers, and the effect was still visible after two weeks. Source By three months the effect was no longer statistically significant, showing that awareness is a modest and decaying intervention rather than immunity. Source The practical resolution is periodic reinforcement plus structural guardrails rather than a one-time lesson. Source Source

  • Is a bias always harmful? The conventional disposition-effect evidence treats premature winner sales and delayed loss realization as costly, yet the 2024 experiment found that a higher disposition effect was associated with higher returns in mean-reverting conditions. Source Source A behavior can be biased in origin without producing the same return consequence in every market regime, so context matters when designing a corrective rule. Source

Unresolved questions:

  • Which fine-grained neural and cognitive mechanisms distinguish otherwise similar behavioral biases in live investment decisions?

  • How do Korea-specific tax rules and account structures interact with turnover, loss realization, and the disposition effect?

9.2 Discipline, process & combining lenses

An investment method has value only if the investor can execute it consistently through fear, euphoria, and uncertainty. Farnam Street Buffett frames the requirement as temperament—the ability to control impulses that get other investors into trouble—rather than exceptional intelligence once ordinary competence is present. Farnam Street Discipline should therefore be designed into written rules, records, and review triggers before the emotional moment arrives. CFA Institute

The durable-process toolkit includes an Investment Policy Statement, a decision journal, checklists, and a pre-mortem. CFA Institute These devices convert vague intentions into actions that can be audited after the fact. CFA Institute The second part of the process assigns different analytical lenses explicit jobs and defines what happens when they disagree. NewTrading ⚠️

Write an Investment Policy Statement

An IPS is a strategic guide for planning and implementing an investment program. CFA Institute It anticipates governance, objectives, asset allocation, implementation, monitoring, risk management, rebalancing, and reporting before markets force a hurried decision. CFA Institute Its most important role is to provide an objective course of action during market disruption when instinctive responses might otherwise lead to imprudent behavior. CFA Institute

CFA Institute organizes an individual IPS into four sections: Scope and Purpose; Governance; Investment, Return, and Risk Objectives; and Risk Management, including the rebalancing process. CFA Institute For a retail investor, those sections can record goals, horizon, liquidity, tax and account constraints, target allocation and ranges, benchmark, contribution rule, maximum active risk, rebalancing method, and the authority to change the plan. CFA Institute

The IPS must be customized to the individual’s preferences, attitudes, constraints, and circumstances. CFA Institute CFA cautions that generic templates sacrifice consideration of factors that may be highly relevant to the investor. CFA Institute A template can provide headings, but the actual thresholds and rules must come from the investor’s goals and capacity. CFA Institute

The document should also contain structured review triggers rather than remain permanently rigid. CFA Institute Material changes in goals, horizon, liquidity, risk capacity, tax situation, or the evidence behind a strategy can justify a prospective amendment. CFA Institute A market decline alone should not silently rewrite the policy in the middle of stress. CFA Institute

Keep a decision journal

A decision or trade journal should record the investor’s emotional state, action, and whether the action followed written rules. WealthBee ⚠️ It should also preserve the thesis, expected catalyst, key evidence, disconfirming conditions, valuation or price range, and the information available at the time. WealthBee ⚠️ Factual, dated entries can be reviewed, whereas vague labels and retrospective stories cannot be tested meaningfully. WealthBee ⚠️

The journal should be written before or immediately after the decision and never rewritten to match the outcome. WealthBee ⚠️ Later review can separate a good process with a poor outcome from a weak process that happened to profit. WealthBee ⚠️ Logging only winning decisions creates another selected dataset and defeats the purpose of the record. WealthBee ⚠️

Use checklists for recurring failure modes

Pabrai reportedly developed his investment checklist after learning from Atul Gawande’s The Checklist Manifesto. Market Folly ⚠️ His list contains roughly 98 questions spanning leverage and debt covenants, competitive moats, labor relations, management, and ownership. Market Folly ⚠️ He reports that, in almost every losing investment whose cause he could identify, the reason was visible before the investment was made. Market Folly ⚠️

The practical lesson is to build a personal checklist from repeated mistakes and known failure conditions. Market Folly ⚠️ Useful items are binary yes-or-no questions that can be run quickly and produce a clear escalation or rejection decision rather than another vague opinion. Snappchart ⚠️ The checklist can cover leverage, dilution, customer concentration, accounting quality, valuation assumptions, liquidity, thesis falsifiers, position size, and conflicts with the IPS. Market Folly ⚠️

A secondary report claims that after adopting the checklist, Pabrai invested about USD 200 million across more than thirty positions, exited with about USD 500 million, and lost money on only two. AAII ⚠️ This medium-confidence anecdote does not establish that the checklist caused the performance. AAII ⚠️ The stronger use of the example is process design: a checklist helps an expert avoid repeating visible errors but does not substitute for judgment. Market Folly ⚠️

Run a pre-mortem before commitment

A pre-mortem imagines that the decision has already failed spectacularly and then asks what caused the failure. Steven Goldstein ⚠️ The exercise uses prospective hindsight to surface risks, missing evidence, and concerns that group enthusiasm or confirmation bias might suppress. Steven Goldstein ⚠️

The cited summary reports that prospective hindsight improves correct identification of reasons for future outcomes by about 30%. Steven Goldstein ⚠️ Because that figure comes through a medium-confidence blog source, it should motivate the exercise rather than be treated as a precise investing-effect estimate. Steven Goldstein ⚠️ A useful pre-mortem converts each plausible failure into a fact to investigate, a position limit, an insurance decision, or a thesis-review trigger. Steven Goldstein ⚠️

Assign fundamental and technical analysis different jobs

The common practitioner division of labor is that fundamental analysis decides what to buy and technical analysis helps decide when to buy. NewTrading ⚠️ The fundamental lens examines business quality, earnings, cash flow, balance sheet, competitive position, and valuation, while the technical lens examines price trend, support, resistance, and timing. NewTrading ⚠️ The division prevents a chart from becoming a substitute for business value and prevents a valuation estimate from ignoring market behavior entirely. NewTrading ⚠️

A concrete combined workflow begins with a fundamental screen using EPS growth, return on equity, debt, and free cash flow to narrow the universe to a manageable list, such as about ten names. NewTrading ⚠️ The investor then uses charts to mark support and resistance zones for possible entry, exit, or invalidation. NewTrading ⚠️ The exact thresholds and chart rules must be written before the outcome so the second lens does not simply rationalize the first. NewTrading ⚠️

The time horizon should determine the dominant lens. NewTrading ⚠️ A long-term business owner should give fundamentals and valuation greater weight, while a short-horizon trader whose thesis is price movement should give technical rules greater weight. NewTrading ⚠️ A short-term technical wobble should not automatically overturn a long-term fundamental thesis, and a strong story should not excuse violating a prewritten trading stop. NewTrading ⚠️

Resolve conflicting signals explicitly

When fundamental and technical lenses agree, practitioners treat the agreement as confirmation; when they disagree, the recommended response is to pause and investigate rather than act immediately. Wealthsimple ⚠️ A fundamentally strong company whose price keeps falling may indicate that the market reflects information or risk missing from the analysis. Wealthsimple ⚠️ A rapidly rising price paired with weak fundamentals may instead reflect short-lived enthusiasm. Wealthsimple ⚠️

Dogmatism creates errors on both sides. NewTrading ⚠️ A fundamentals-only investor can ignore information embedded in price action, while a price-action-only trader can hold a severely overvalued security. NewTrading ⚠️ Combining lenses should identify new questions and boundaries, not provide two vocabularies for defending a prior preference. NewTrading ⚠️

One low-confidence tactic is to reduce position size when signals conflict and increase it when analysis provides clearer conviction. Korynexa ⚠️ The sizing principle is directionally cautious but should not be treated as validated evidence that subjective conviction forecasts return. Korynexa ⚠️ A position limit should remain bounded by portfolio risk even when both lenses agree. Korynexa ⚠️

The alternative is to remove discretionary reconciliation. Enlightened Stock Trading ⚠️ The mechanization school recommends adopting a systematic, backtested rule set that triggers only on defined conditions and ignores non-triggering indicators. Enlightened Stock Trading ⚠️ This can reduce emotional signal-switching, but a backtested system can still be overfit and requires independent validation and retirement rules. Enlightened Stock Trading ⚠️

Who needs the most process

The marginal value of explicit discipline rises with the number of discretionary decisions a strategy requires. CFA Institute Active stock pickers, concentrated investors, and swing or position traders face more moments in which emotion can alter the plan, while a passive investor can automate much of the policy. CFA Institute Automation is a form of precommitment when it implements an allocation and contribution rule already selected through the IPS. CFA Institute

Combining lenses is most relevant to investors who both select securities and choose entry or exit timing. NewTrading ⚠️ A pure index investor does not need technical analysis to implement a long-horizon allocation, while a very short-horizon trader may not use company fundamentals as the primary signal. NewTrading ⚠️ More inputs are not automatically better because excessive indicators can create false precision and analysis paralysis. NewTrading ⚠️

A durable operating loop

  1. Write a customized IPS covering scope, governance, goals, return and risk objectives, allocation, rebalancing, and risk management. CFA Institute
  2. Before each discretionary decision, record the thesis, catalyst, evidence, emotional state, rules, and falsifiers in a dated journal. WealthBee ⚠️
  3. Run a short binary checklist built from prior errors and the strategy’s recurring failure modes. Snappchart ⚠️
  4. Perform a pre-mortem and convert imagined causes of failure into research questions, limits, and review triggers. Steven Goldstein ⚠️
  5. Assign each analytical lens an explicit job and make the time horizon determine which lens has authority. NewTrading ⚠️
  6. When signals conflict, pause, seek the missing evidence, reduce or avoid the position, or apply a prevalidated systematic rule rather than switching explanations opportunistically. Wealthsimple ⚠️
  7. Review outcomes separately from process adherence and amend the IPS or strategy only through a prospective, documented review. CFA Institute

Tensions:

  • Discretionary reconciliation versus mechanization. One camp uses judgment: let horizon set priority, treat agreement as confirmation, pause on disagreement, and investigate before sizing the trade. Wealthsimple ⚠️ The other camp rejects ad hoc reconciliation and uses one backtested rule set to remove emotional signal switching. Enlightened Stock Trading ⚠️ Both seek consistency but disagree about whether structured human judgment belongs in the loop. Enlightened Stock Trading ⚠️
  • Combining lenses versus diluting each method. Practitioners commonly recommend fundamentals for selection and technicals for timing, but the node found no rigorous evidence that the hybrid outperforms either lens alone. NewTrading ⚠️ Additional lenses may provide useful disconfirming questions or may create curve-fitting and a rationale for whatever action the investor already preferred. NewTrading ⚠️
  • Rigid policy versus necessary flexibility. A precommitted IPS protects against impulsive action during market disruption, but permanent blind rule-following can preserve a broken thesis. CFA Institute The resolution is to predefine structured thesis-review and policy-amendment triggers rather than choose between no rules and immutable rules. CFA Institute

Unresolved questions:

  • Does a fundamentals-plus-technicals process outperform either lens alone after costs and out-of-sample testing?
  • What fully worked line-by-line IPS template best fits a self-directed retail investor without becoming generic or bureaucratic?
  • Which Korea-specific automation, robo-advisor, and account mechanisms most effectively implement the IPS while preserving needed flexibility?

9.3 Retail crowd & social dynamics

Social investing networks can distribute both useful ideas and costly errors at a scale that an isolated investor cannot create. Finfluencers paper Research on more than 29,000 StockTwits finfluencers found that reach was not aligned with skill, while enforcement cases show that promoters can use social platforms to buy, tout, and then sell into follower demand. Finfluencers paper SEC The learner’s central task is therefore information hygiene: evaluate the source, the source’s incentives, the evidence behind the claim, and the market conditions before treating popularity as investment merit. Finfluencers paper SEC

This problem is increasingly relevant because social media is a routine information source for retail investors. FINRA FINRA reports that 24% of investors obtain investing information from social media, with usage at 35% among respondents under 30 and 13% among those aged 65 or older. FINRA The age gradient makes crowd dynamics especially important for newer investors whose first encounter with markets may occur inside a feed rather than through filings or formal research. FINRA

Why engagement and expertise diverge

The strongest evidence in this node comes from the StockTwits finfluencer study. Finfluencers paper It classifies 28% of finfluencers as skilled, producing 2.6% monthly abnormal returns, 16% as unskilled, and 56% as antiskilled, producing -2.3% monthly abnormal returns. Finfluencers paper The striking finding is not merely that the antiskilled group is the largest, but that it attracts more followers and has more influence on retail trading than the skilled group. Finfluencers paper

The study links antiskilled advice to excessively optimistic beliefs, persistent swings in follower beliefs, excessive trading, and inefficient prices. Finfluencers paper A contrarian strategy against antiskilled recommendations produced a reported 1.2% monthly out-of-sample return. Finfluencers paper That result is evidence about a systematic research design, not an invitation for a learner to short every popular post without replicating the classification and execution method. Finfluencers paper

The practical implication is that follower count and engagement are poor substitutes for a verified track record. Finfluencers paper A large audience can reflect content that is more optimistic or engaging rather than more accurate. Finfluencers paper The data also complicate the claim that every finfluencer is fraudulent or incompetent, because the same study identifies a genuinely skilled 28% minority. Finfluencers paper The defensive lesson is selective skepticism rather than automatic belief or blanket dismissal. Finfluencers paper

How a meme-stock crowd becomes market force

GameStop in January 2021 provides the clearest market-plumbing example in the evidence set. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze Approximately 140% of its public float had been sold short on January 22, a level reported to have exceeded 100% only 15 times in the prior decade. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze A stock with such extreme short positioning can rise as short sellers buy shares to close their positions, and those purchases can add to demand. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze

The popular gamma-squeeze explanation adds options dealers to the feedback loop. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze Under that account, retail call-option purchases cause call sellers to hedge by buying the underlying shares, which pushes the price higher and can require additional hedging. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze The evidence record later contests whether this mechanism materially drove GameStop, so it should be understood as a proposed mechanism rather than a settled description of the episode. WealthManagement ⚠️

The observed price path was extreme. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze GameStop rose about 1,500% over two weeks, traded above $500 before the market, and then lost more than 80% from its peak by February 1–2. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze Robinhood restricted purchases on January 28 after industrywide clearinghouse collateral requirements increased from $26 billion to $33.5 billion. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze The episode shows that social demand, short positioning, options activity, brokerage capacity, and clearing requirements can all matter to the trading path. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze

The crowd itself expanded with the event. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze A medium-confidence passage reports that r/WallStreetBets received 73 million page views in 24 hours and grew by 1.5 million members overnight to about 6 million. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze ⚠️ The figures illustrate reflexivity between attention and price, but the node does not isolate whether the new attention caused later buying or merely followed the price move. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze ⚠️

The May 2024 replay was faster. CNBC Keith Gill’s first social-media post since 2021 coincided with both GameStop and AMC rising more than 70% on May 13. CNBC The one-day response demonstrates how a known social signal can rapidly concentrate attention, although it does not establish a durable valuation change. CNBC

What actually drove GameStop remains contested

A medium-confidence account of the SEC’s 2021 staff report says the regulator found no evidence of a gamma squeeze and concluded that positive sentiment, rather than short sellers buying to cover, sustained the weeks-long appreciation. WealthManagement ⚠️ The same source describes later academic criticism arguing that a short squeeze played a larger role and that the SEC did not examine relevant securities-lending data. WealthManagement ⚠️ Both interpretations accept that crowd attention mattered, but they assign different weight to sentiment, short covering, and options-market hedging. WealthManagement ⚠️

For a learner, this disagreement is a warning against reducing a complex episode to one viral explanation. WealthManagement ⚠️ A high short-interest figure does not by itself prove that covering caused the full price rise, and heavy call activity does not by itself prove a dominant gamma squeeze. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze WealthManagement ⚠️ The appropriate conclusion from this node is that social coordination interacted with market plumbing, while the relative contribution of each mechanism remains disputed. WealthManagement ⚠️

Pump-and-dump and scalping incentives

Social manipulation converts audience trust into trading liquidity. SEC A promoter can acquire a position, publicly tout the security, and then sell into the buying response without disclosing the intent to exit. SEC A medium-confidence SEC investor alert describes pump-and-dump schemes as typically involving small microcap companies, false or misleading statements, a buying frenzy, and promoters selling before the hype ends while remaining investors suffer losses. SEC investor alert ⚠️

The Atlas Trading enforcement case shows the possible scale. SEC In December 2022, the SEC charged eight social-media influencers with operating an approximately $100 million securities-fraud scheme through Twitter and a Discord room with more than 233,000 members, while reaching more than 1.5 million followers and selling promoted securities without disclosing plans to dump them. SEC The Department of Justice separately stated that the alleged scheme ran until about April 2022 and produced at least $114 million in profits. US Department of Justice

The conflict of interest is structural: followers are encouraged to create demand for a security the promoter already holds and intends to sell. SEC A follower who evaluates only the stated price target misses the more important question of how the speaker benefits from the audience’s action. SEC Position disclosure, paid-promotion disclosure, and independently verifiable evidence are therefore essential checks before acting on a social recommendation. SEC

Knowledge, confidence, and fraud exposure

Medium-confidence FINRA Foundation research reports a large confidence gap among social-media investors. FINRA Foundation ⚠️ Social-media users answered 42% of objective investment-knowledge questions correctly on average, yet 63% rated their knowledge as high. FINRA Foundation ⚠️ The comparison suggests that frequent access to investing content can coexist with overconfidence about actual understanding. FINRA Foundation ⚠️

The same source reports that 69% of targeted finfluencer followers who encountered fraud lost money, compared with 26% among non-followers. FINRA Foundation ⚠️ This association does not prove that following a finfluencer caused every loss, but it identifies a materially worse reported outcome among the targeted follower group. FINRA Foundation ⚠️ Combined with the StockTwits evidence, it supports skepticism toward popularity as either a knowledge credential or a safety signal. FINRA Foundation ⚠️ Finfluencers paper

A practical information-hygiene routine

The first check is identity and incentive. SEC The learner should establish who is speaking, whether the person holds the promoted security, whether compensation is involved, and whether an intended sale is disclosed. SEC The Atlas case demonstrates why a public statement of enthusiasm cannot be assumed to describe the promoter’s actual trading plan. SEC

The second check is evidence quality. Finfluencers paper A claim supported only by audience size or engagement should not receive the same weight as one supported by primary filings and a reproducible analysis, because the strongest study here finds that antiskilled voices have more followers and influence. Finfluencers paper The existence of skilled finfluencers means source review should discriminate among speakers rather than reject the entire medium. Finfluencers paper

The third check is manipulation language. Kiplinger ⚠️ A medium-confidence consumer article identifies promises above roughly 10% annually, flaunted wealth, high-pressure tactics by unregistered people, quick-profit claims, guaranteed returns, and hype around thinly traded small-float stocks as red flags. Kiplinger ⚠️ These flags do not prove fraud individually, but they indicate when independent verification and restraint should become more demanding. Kiplinger ⚠️

The fourth check is timing. CNBC A signal that already produced a same-day price move of more than 70% in GameStop and AMC is no longer the same opportunity that existed before the post. CNBC The 2021 GameStop path also shows that a rapid 1,500% rise can be followed within days by a decline greater than 80% from the peak. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze Social proof after a large move is therefore not evidence that the late buyer has the same payoff as the early participant. CNBC Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze

The fifth check is decision independence. Finfluencers paper The investor should restate the thesis without the influencer’s identity, follower count, slogans, or price excitement and ask whether the remaining evidence still supports the trade. Finfluencers paper If the only remaining rationale is that many people are watching, the decision depends on crowd continuation rather than demonstrated skill or independently verified value. Finfluencers paper

Korea’s 리딩방 problem

Korea’s regulator has documented front-running through SNS stock-leading rooms. Korea Financial Services Commission Operators bought shares shortly before recommending them, then sold after follower purchases lifted the price, with the conduct spanning more than 700 stocks over several years and violating Capital Markets Act Article 178. Korea Financial Services Commission The pattern mirrors the incentive conflict in the US Atlas case: the promoter’s pre-positioning turns follower demand into an exit opportunity. Korea Financial Services Commission SEC

A medium-confidence Korean source says a 2024 Capital Markets Act revision made it illegal to operate a paid leading room that recommends investment products or real-time strategies through open chat rooms or Telegram without proper licensing. Toss Bank ⚠️ The licensing claim should be verified against the applicable regulatory record before being used as legal advice, because the evidence here is secondary. Toss Bank ⚠️ The primary enforcement evidence nevertheless establishes that buying before a recommendation and selling into follower demand has been sanctioned as unlawful conduct. Korea Financial Services Commission

What a learner should and should not infer

Social media can surface skilled voices, because 28% of the studied finfluencers were classified as skilled and generated positive abnormal returns in the sample. Finfluencers paper It can also expose users to a negatively selected influence structure, because the antiskilled majority attracted more followers and affected more retail trading. Finfluencers paper The rational response is neither automatic imitation nor total abstention, but a verification process that discounts popularity and examines incentives. Finfluencers paper SEC

Meme-stock episodes also prove that social attention can coincide with very large short-term price moves. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze CNBC They do not prove that a late follower has positive expected return, that the public squeeze narrative identifies the dominant mechanism, or that a dramatic price move represents a durable change in fundamental value. WealthManagement ⚠️ Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze The defensive use of this node is to identify when social coordination changes trading conditions without confusing that change with an investment thesis. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze Finfluencers paper

Tensions:

The first tension concerns what drove GameStop in 2021. WealthManagement ⚠️ The popular account emphasizes short covering and dealer hedging, supported by the extreme 140% short interest and the described gamma-squeeze mechanism. Wikipedia, GameStop short squeeze A medium-confidence account of the SEC report says it found no gamma-squeeze evidence and attributed sustained appreciation to positive sentiment rather than covering, while later academics argued that short lending data would support a larger short-squeeze role. WealthManagement ⚠️ Social coordination is common to both explanations, but the dominant plumbing mechanism remains unsettled in this evidence. WealthManagement ⚠️

The second tension is whether social media is net harmful or democratizing. FINRA Finfluencers paper Social platforms are used by 24% of investors in the FINRA evidence and especially by younger respondents, which makes information broadly accessible. FINRA Yet the finfluencer study finds a 56% antiskilled majority with greater reach, and medium-confidence FINRA Foundation evidence reports a knowledge-confidence gap and higher fraud-loss incidence among targeted followers. Finfluencers paper FINRA Foundation ⚠️ The platform can lower access barriers while still producing a poor average influence structure. FINRA Finfluencers paper

The third tension is the slogan that all finfluencers are scams. Finfluencers paper The data reject the universal claim because 28% were skilled, but they also show that skill was negatively related to influence because antiskilled accounts had more followers and impact. Finfluencers paper The correct filter is not whether a speaker uses social media, but whether skill, incentives, disclosures, and evidence can be independently established. Finfluencers paper SEC

Unresolved questions:

  • How quickly does any systematic edge from meme momentum or social-sentiment signals decay after message volume or price attention spikes, and what survives realistic execution costs? CNBC Finfluencers paper
  • What is the precise scale and distribution of Korean retail losses attributable to meme-style trading and SNS 리딩방 activity, beyond the primary enforcement evidence covering more than 700 front-run recommendations? Korea Financial Services Commission
  • How reliable are AI and large-language-model research tools that summarize social sentiment, and do they reduce or amplify the engagement-over-skill problem identified in the finfluencer evidence? Finfluencers paper

10. Tools, vehicles & access

The orthogonal delivery axis: how modern investing actually happens — research tooling and automated/managed implementations.

10.1 Idea generation, screening & research tooling

Research tooling is a funnel for allocating attention, not an oracle that turns a screen result into a buy decision. Lo and MacKinlay Its job is to reduce a large investable universe to a small, auditable research queue, establish facts from primary disclosures, and keep each thesis current as new evidence arrives. Investor.gov A good output is not a ticker list but a reproducible packet containing a dated thesis, valuation range, key assumptions, KPI history, risks, disconfirming evidence, source links, and an explicit next action. Investor.gov

The governing source hierarchy puts official company filings and disclosure systems first, original investor-relations materials second, normalized data services third, and commentary or machine-generated summaries last. Investor.gov Structured databases and AI can accelerate search, extraction, comparison, and drafting, but neither replaces the filing as the source of record. NIST

Start with a hypothesis, not a filter menu

The research question should be written before the screen, including the economic reason a characteristic might matter and the observations that would falsify the idea. Lo and MacKinlay Examples can include profitable compounders at reasonable prices, balance-sheet repair, or high-return businesses experiencing temporary margin pressure, but every hypothesis needs its own evidence and rejection conditions. Lo and MacKinlay Constructing a sorting rule from patterns discovered in the same dataset and then testing it on that dataset can create substantial data-snooping bias. Lo and MacKinlay

The investable universe should specify country, exchange, currency, market capitalization, share price, free float, trading liquidity, listing history, sector, and accounting-data availability. Lo and MacKinlay Historical evaluation must retain delisted securities and use the values and constituents known at the time rather than today’s survivors or later-restated data. Lo and MacKinlay Securities the investor cannot trade, understand, or monitor should be removed through predeclared investability rules rather than discretionary cleanup after seeing returns. Lo and MacKinlay

Screening works best in layers. Lo and MacKinlay Hard investability and balance-sheet exclusions come first, followed by hypothesis-relevant measures such as growth, margins, return on capital, cash conversion, leverage, dilution, and valuation. Investor.gov Broad ranks or bands are generally more auditable than knife-edge thresholds, and names just outside a cutoff should be inspected for sensitivity and data errors. Lo and MacKinlay Sector-specific measures belong only where the underlying business definitions have been reconciled across firms and periods. Investor.gov

A screen creates a research queue, not a portfolio

Every survivor should receive a short primary-source triage before it consumes deep-research time. Investor.gov That check covers the business description, revenue segments, debt, cash flow, share count, auditor opinion, recent material events, and whether an exceptional or stale number produced the screen result. Investor.gov Obvious mismatches are rejected with a dated reason so the funnel can later be evaluated for false positives and repeated mistakes. Lo and MacKinlay

A useful watchlist records the ticker and stable issuer identifier, the one-sentence thesis, screen and source dates, valuation range, current price, key KPIs, red flags, catalysts, falsifiers, next reporting date, links, and last review. SEC EDGAR APIs Each record should have one explicit status such as reject, watch, investigate, or eligible for a separate sizing process. Investor.gov Limiting the active queue prevents idea collection and dashboard building from displacing analysis and decisions. Lo and MacKinlay

Funnel metrics make the process reviewable. Lo and MacKinlay Useful measures include universe size, survivor count, pass rate, reject reasons, research hours per accepted candidate, record age, and conversion from screen to deep dive, watchlist, and decision. Lo and MacKinlay These measures evaluate accuracy, coverage, reproducibility, and attention allocation rather than claiming that the research tool itself generates alpha. Lo and MacKinlay

Build the factual base from filings

The 10-K is normally more detailed than the glossy annual report sent to shareholders and provides the base document for understanding a U.S. issuer. Investor.gov Investor.gov identifies the Business section as a good starting place for learning how the company operates. Investor.gov A complete read then connects Risk Factors, MD&A, audited statements and notes, the auditor report, controls, material contracts, and segment information. Investor.gov

MD&A contains known trends, liquidity and capital resources, material period-to-period changes, and critical accounting judgments whose estimates and assumptions can materially affect the statements. Investor.gov The analyst should compare several years and search the filing and footnotes for customer concentration, covenants, related parties, impairments, restatements, litigation, stock compensation, dilution, and non-GAAP reconciliation. Investor.gov The CEO and CFO certify the accuracy of Forms 10-K and 10-Q, but the SEC explicitly does not vouch for their accuracy. Investor.gov

A 10-Q is a more abbreviated quarterly update to the 10-K and includes financial statements, MD&A, risk, controls, and legal-proceeding items. Investor.gov Efficient monitoring reads the delta: changed risks, cash flow, working capital, assumptions, guidance, and footnotes are compared with the base annual filing rather than rediscovered from zero each quarter. Investor.gov

Form 8-K is the current-event stream for information a reasonable investor would consider important, and most covered disclosures are due within four business days of the triggering event. Investor.gov Earnings, financing, acquisitions, impairments, leadership or auditor changes, defaults, and delisting matters should therefore enter the watchlist through 8-K monitoring rather than waiting for the next periodic report. Investor.gov Earnings releases are often attached as 8-K exhibits, while the full financial statements normally arrive later in the 10-Q or 10-K. Investor.gov

Accession numbers, filing dates, amendments, units, periods, and direct links should be preserved so another researcher can reproduce the evidence trail. SEC EDGAR APIs A thesis update should record only what changed in the estimate, valuation, risk, catalyst, confidence, or conclusion while retaining prior versions rather than rewriting history. Investor.gov

Use structured data as an index and calculation layer

SEC data APIs provide issuer submission histories and XBRL financial-statement data in JSON without authentication or API keys. SEC EDGAR APIs Their JSON structures are updated throughout the day in real time as submissions are disseminated. SEC EDGAR APIs The Company Facts endpoint can return all standardized company-concept data for one issuer in a single call, enabling scalable histories and cross-checks. SEC EDGAR APIs

Machine readability does not make every value economically comparable. SEC EDGAR APIs Companies can extend standard XBRL taxonomies with custom tags, and facts still carry context such as units, periods, dimensions, amendments, and consolidated versus standalone scope. SEC EDGAR APIs High-impact values should be reconciled to the filing, footnote, tagged fact, latest amendment, and the company’s definition before entering a valuation model. SEC EDGAR APIs

Data-quality controls should check annual versus quarterly duration, original versus amended filings, GAAP or K-IFRS versus adjusted measures, splits, currencies, fiscal dates, restatements, missing values, custom tags, and vendor-to-filing differences. SEC EDGAR APIs A normalized vendor database can accelerate comparison, but formula, sign, unit, and period-mapping choices must be audited against primary evidence. SEC EDGAR APIs

Run earnings calls as a controlled update loop

Before an earnings call, the analyst should write expected KPIs, assumptions requiring confirmation, and unanswered questions. SEC Regulation FD guidance A public earnings call used for Regulation FD should be announced with its date, time, subject matter, and access information, and the SEC encourages issuers to explain how and for how long a transcript or replay will remain available. SEC Regulation FD guidance The analyst can therefore preserve the official webcast, slides, or replay as the verification source for a third-party transcript. SEC Regulation FD guidance

After the release, GAAP results, non-GAAP adjustments, guidance, and the 8-K exhibit should be reconciled before interpreting management’s narrative. Investor.gov Prepared remarks and analyst Q&A should be separated because they reflect different degrees of management control and challenge. Earnings-call study Peer-reviewed evidence finds that Q&A tone adds explanatory power for post-earnings-announcement drift and that an earnings-specific language dictionary detects relevant tone better than a generic dictionary. Earnings-call study

That evidence does not make transcript sentiment a stand-alone trading rule. Earnings-call study Earnings calls are unstructured and conversational, and their meaning depends on speakers, transcription, metric definitions, firm uncertainty, and sample design. ACL Industry 2026 The durable use of the call is to record changed wording, challenged assumptions, management non-answers, revised KPI definitions, and the thesis delta while verifying load-bearing language against the official record. SEC Regulation FD guidance

Use generative AI as a cited copilot

AI is most useful for bounded and verifiable tasks such as locating a clause, extracting a table, comparing supplied filings, summarizing a supplied transcript, drafting a checklist, or generating code that the analyst tests. NIST The prompt should provide the trusted document set and require the accession or page, direct quote, unit, period, and an explicit “not found” response when evidence is absent. NIST NIST defines generative-AI confabulation as confidently stated erroneous or false content that can mislead users and recommends reviewing and verifying generated sources and citations. NIST

Financial benchmark results show why fluent output cannot be trusted by appearance. FinanceBench In the original FinanceBench evaluation, GPT-4-Turbo with retrieval answered incorrectly or refused 81% of 150 reviewed financial questions, and the authors observed hallucination weaknesses across every tested model configuration. FinanceBench A 2026 ACL system achieved 79.7% human-verified precision when extracting emerging KPIs from unstructured earnings-call transcripts, demonstrating useful productivity but leaving too much residual error for unattended financial decisions. ACL Industry 2026

Reproducibility matters as much as point accuracy. Withdrawn working paper A widely circulated working paper claiming that ChatGPT summaries helped investors was withdrawn after a coauthor could not independently reproduce its key findings. Withdrawn working paper An AI-assisted workflow should therefore log the source set, prompt, model and version, date, sample, extraction accuracy, numerical error rate, unsupported-claim rate, abstention rate, and manual corrections. NIST

Arithmetic should be recalculated in a spreadsheet or code rather than accepted from a language model, and a sample of extracted values should be opened in the original document. NIST Prompts should request counterarguments, missing evidence, and contradictions as well as the attractive case so the tool does not merely amplify confirmation bias. NIST Final valuation assumptions and buy, sell, or sizing decisions remain human responsibilities outside the research copilot. NIST

Korea research stack

OpenDART permits users to obtain original Korean disclosure reports through an API and download them in XML. OpenDART It also exposes annual-report items, major financial accounts, ownership reports, major-event reports, and registration-statement information in machine-usable form. OpenDART That makes DART/OpenDART the primary source and structured-data layer for Korean issuer research, while issuer materials and other platforms remain discovery or convenience tools. OpenDART

Korean research records should preserve the company’s receipt number, correction filings, reporting scope, units, periods, and the original Korean text used for any material conclusion. OpenDART Machine translation, normalized databases, and AI summaries should be verified against that source because structured access does not eliminate amendments, definition differences, or extraction error. OpenDART

A ten-step operating loop

  1. Write the hypothesis and its falsifiers before choosing fields or looking at historical screen returns. Lo and MacKinlay
  2. Define an investable universe with point-in-time, survivor-aware data and explicit liquidity, listing, sector, and accounting rules. Lo and MacKinlay
  3. Apply layered, thesis-relevant filters and inspect near-threshold names instead of treating a decimal cutoff as economic truth. Lo and MacKinlay
  4. Triage each survivor against its primary filing and reject stale, exceptional, or misunderstood inputs before deep work. Investor.gov
  5. Add accepted candidates to a capped, dated queue containing thesis, valuation, KPIs, risks, falsifiers, catalysts, links, and next action. SEC EDGAR APIs
  6. Read the base 10-K across Business, Risk Factors, MD&A, audited statements, notes, controls, and auditor material. Investor.gov
  7. Monitor 10-Q changes and the 8-K event stream while preserving accession numbers and amendments. Investor.gov
  8. Prepare KPI questions before earnings, reconcile the release and filing, separate prepared remarks from Q&A, and verify transcripts against official materials. Earnings-call study
  9. Record only the thesis delta while retaining prior versions and the underlying quote, period, unit, and source. SEC EDGAR APIs
  10. Use AI only on a supplied evidence set, require traceable citations and abstention, recalculate numbers independently, and keep final judgment human. NIST

Tensions:

  • Screener insight versus attention compression. A hypothesis-led screen can enforce consistent definitions and surface unfamiliar candidates, but constructing rules from observed data can create substantial data-snooping bias. Lo and MacKinlay The screen is most defensible as a reproducible research queue rather than evidence of future return. Lo and MacKinlay
  • Structured data versus document reading. EDGAR APIs provide scalable real-time JSON and XBRL histories without authentication, but companies can use custom tags and the SEC does not guarantee filing accuracy. SEC EDGAR APIs Investor.gov Structured data should serve as the index and calculation layer, while material values remain tied to the filing context, footnote, unit, period, and latest amendment. SEC EDGAR APIs
  • Earnings-call information versus sentiment overreach. Q&A tone has incremental explanatory power and finance-specific language outperformed a generic dictionary in the cited study. Earnings-call study Calls remain unstructured communications without built-in labels, so the evidence supports careful question and KPI review rather than a mechanical tone trade. ACL Industry 2026
  • AI productivity versus financial reliability. Bounded KPI extraction reached 79.7% human-verified precision in a 2026 study, while FinanceBench found an earlier retrieval-equipped configuration wrong or refusing 81% of reviewed questions. ACL Industry 2026 FinanceBench NIST’s confabulation risk and the withdrawn replication failure show why task, model, retrieval set, citations, arithmetic, and version must all be independently verified. NIST Withdrawn working paper

Unresolved questions:

  • Which screener formula and ranking logic best match the investor’s specific strategy, sector universe, market, history, and workflow?
  • Which current data or research vendor provides enough coverage, point-in-time history, normalization, exports, and licensing value to justify its live price?

10.2 Robo-advisors & automated/managed portfolios

Robo-advisers, target-date funds, and model portfolios are implementation systems rather than distinct return-producing asset classes. Investor.gov They translate client information or a retirement date into an allocation, then automate some combination of portfolio construction, rebalancing, tax actions, and administration. Investor.gov Department of Labor Their strongest evidence concerns better diversification, lower fees, and easier execution for investors who began with weak portfolios, not an algorithmic ability to forecast securities or guarantee outperformance. Rossi and Utkus

The correct comparison is therefore a risk-matched, after-fee outcome against a disciplined self-directed portfolio holding the same underlying allocation. Investor.gov fee bulletin Automation can be valuable when it replaces inertia, poor diversification, expensive holdings, or inconsistent behavior. Rossi and Utkus Its incremental value narrows when the alternative is already a simple, low-cost, well-maintained index portfolio. Rossi and Utkus

How a robo-adviser works

A robo-adviser typically begins with a digital questionnaire covering goals, investment horizon, income, assets, and risk tolerance. Investor.gov The platform uses those answers to create and manage an investment portfolio. Investor.gov The recommendation is limited by the information the platform requests and receives, and the client often bears responsibility for keeping that information current. Investor.gov

This intake creates the first major suitability risk. Investor.gov A model cannot incorporate a job loss, new dependent, outside account, debt burden, or changed withdrawal need if those facts were never requested or updated. Investor.gov A risk score should therefore be treated as an output of observed questionnaire variables rather than as complete knowledge of the household. Investor.gov

After intake, the system maps the investor to a model allocation and implements it in an account. Investor.gov The client should inspect the portfolio’s stock, bond, cash, domestic, and international exposures and identify whether the service favors affiliated funds or cash arrangements. Investor.gov A low headline advisory fee does not ensure a low total cost when the underlying products are expensive. Investor.gov

Rebalancing restores the model’s target exposure after market moves or cash flows, but it is an allocation-maintenance function rather than a return forecast. Department of Labor A client should understand whether the system rebalances on a schedule, after tolerance bands are breached, or when the provider changes the model. Investor.gov The review should also identify whether new deposits are used before taxable sales and how model changes are communicated. Investor.gov

Tax-loss harvesting is an optional automation rather than a universal source of alpha. Investor.gov Its value depends on the client’s tax situation and can implicate wash-sale rules. Investor.gov The investor should evaluate the household-level after-tax benefit rather than the gross amount of losses harvested, because transactions in other accounts can affect the result. Investor.gov

The advisory agreement remains the controlling client document even when accepted through an app checkbox. Investor.gov subscription-fee bulletin It specifies the services and fee structure and is a legal document. Investor.gov subscription-fee bulletin The client should read it as carefully as a traditional advisory contract rather than assuming a digital interface simplifies the underlying obligations. Investor.gov subscription-fee bulletin

Target-date funds

A target-date fund is a one-fund portfolio built around a selected retirement year. Department of Labor It automatically rebalances toward a more conservative allocation as the target year approaches. Department of Labor This glide path reduces the need for the investor to change the strategic allocation manually. Department of Labor

The year in the fund name does not fully specify its risk. Department of Labor A “to retirement” glide path reaches its most conservative allocation at the target date, while a “through retirement” path reaches its most conservative point years later. Department of Labor Funds with the same target year can differ substantially in investment strategy, glide path, and fees. Department of Labor

These differences become visible during stress. GAO GAO reported that funds nearer their target dates had smaller losses than distant vintages in March 2020, but performance varied more among the near-date funds. GAO As of April 2026, GAO still recommended updated guidance covering collective investment trusts and the distinction between “to” and “through” glide paths. GAO

A target-date fund is therefore a population-level default rather than a complete individualized retirement plan. Department of Labor The investor should inspect the current equity allocation, the allocation at retirement, the terminal allocation, the underlying funds, and the total fee. Department of Labor Adding extra stock or bond funds alongside it changes the intended household allocation even though the target-date fund continues to rebalance internally. Department of Labor

Model portfolios and managed accounts

A model portfolio separates portfolio design from some or all of the client-facing implementation. SEC model-portfolio request Third-party model use can obscure which services the model provider and the client-facing adviser each perform. SEC model-portfolio request It can also create uncertainty about fees, conflicts, and which party owes the client a fiduciary duty. SEC model-portfolio request

The client should ask who designs the allocation, who decides whether it is suitable, who has trading discretion, who monitors household changes, and who corrects implementation errors. SEC model-portfolio request A centrally updated model can promote consistency, but the benefit depends on changes reaching client accounts accurately and on responsibilities being clearly assigned. SEC model-portfolio request A methodology document and responsibility map are therefore as important as the model’s historical return. SEC model-portfolio request

Wrap programs create another layer of implementation. Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin A wrap account bundles investment management and brokerage, but its total fee can be higher or lower than purchasing the services separately. Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin Clients may still pay mutual-fund expenses and brokerage costs for trades executed outside the wrap program. Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin

This means a bundled fee must be evaluated against actual account activity. Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin A low-turnover portfolio may receive little brokerage value from the bundle, while a client using planning, administration, and frequent service may value more of it. Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin The relevant number is the total annual dollars paid for the services actually used, not the marketing label attached to the program. Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin

Cost analysis

Fees compound against the investor over long horizons. Investor.gov fee bulletin The SEC’s 2025 illustration shows $100,000 growing at 4% for 20 years ending near $208,000 after a 0.25% annual fee, $198,000 after 0.50%, and $179,000 after 1.00%. Investor.gov fee bulletin The difference arises even though the assumed pre-fee return is the same in each case. Investor.gov fee bulletin

Subscription pricing can be especially expensive on small balances. Investor.gov subscription-fee bulletin A $3 monthly charge on a $500 account is $36 per year, exceeding 7% of the account value before investment-product costs. Investor.gov subscription-fee bulletin Converting every flat fee into a percentage of the current account balance makes the burden comparable with an asset-based fee. Investor.gov subscription-fee bulletin

The all-in cost is broader than the advertised advice charge. Investor.gov Underlying fund expenses can keep the total cost high even when the robo fee is low, and wrap clients can incur additional fund or outside-brokerage costs. Investor.gov Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin The comparison should therefore include the advisory or subscription fee, product expenses, brokerage or spread costs, and any bundled-service charges. Investor.gov wrap-fee bulletin

The DIY counterfactual should hold the asset allocation constant. Investor.gov fee bulletin If the investor can hold the same low-cost funds directly and rebalance with adequate discipline, the incremental robo fee buys convenience, monitoring, tax tools, reporting, or access rather than a different asset-class return. Rossi and Utkus The investor should pay that fee only when the additional service is worth more than its long-run compounded cost. Investor.gov fee bulletin

Evidence on investor outcomes

The strongest cited outcome study concerns a large U.S. hybrid robo-adviser.
